body_preview:
Hey,

Mom called this morning, she's driving up from Williamsburg Thursday afternoon, wants to stay through Sunday. I told her the weekend was fine but I honestly wasn't sure what your Thursday looks like. She's already asking about Nora's spring concert
subject: Hey

body_preview:
Greg,

Thursday I'm back-to-back until 3:45, I can't do pickup. If you leave by 4 you're fine: Owen's out at 3:20, Nora at 3:35. Don't try to do both solo if you're running late, just text Sharon and she can grab Nora.

Saturday at Maymont is fine. Nora
subject: Re: Hey

body_preview:
Nora,

Trying to lock down Grandma Donna's birthday dinner before your father volunteers our house without consulting anyone. I'm thinking Saturday the 21st, 6:30, either here or Stella's if you'd rather not spend the night watching Owen explain somethin
subject: Nora

body_preview:
Mom,

Saturday works, I'll leave by 3:15 to be safe. Last time I hit that stretch I sat for forty minutes and listened to the same podcast twice.

Honestly? Stella's is the right call. No offense to the house but remember Owen's Minecraft presentation at
subject: Re: Nora

body_preview:
Nora,

Yes to flowers, dahlias if they have them, she prefers the grocery store ones anyway, which I've decided is charming. Skip the cake, I'm getting the lemon one from Proper Pie.

Reservation is made at Stella's, 6:30, party of 6. Your dad is already
subject: Re: Nora

body_preview:
Kelly,

I wanted to check in about Owen's dentist appointment. Dr. Kim had a cancellation next Tuesday at 2:30pm — should I go ahead and book it for him? Also, his basketball practice schedule for next week got updated. Coach Martinez moved Thursday's session to Friday at 5pm. Just wanted to make sure you had it.
subject: Owen

body_preview:
Greg,

Yes, book the Tuesday slot with Dr. Kim — Owen is overdue for that cleaning anyway. And good to know about Friday practice, I'll move the grocery run to Thursday evening instead.

Also remind him to bring his mouth guard, he left it in his gym bag last time.
subject: Re: Owen

body_preview:
Okay. Fair. Rewrote paragraph three, new version below. Cut the line too, I know.

Quick question: Renee's daughter went through this last year, right? Did she use an outside counselor or just her school one? My counselor is fine but I don't think she's
subject: Re: Okay. Fair. Rewrote paragraph three, new version below. Cut

body_preview:
Greg,

New paragraph is significantly better. The pacing is right and it sounds like you.

Renee's daughter worked mainly with her school counselor, I think there was one outside session but it wasn't a whole thing. If yours isn't reading closely, ask he
subject: Re: Owen

body_preview:
Donna,

Owen's soccer season wraps up Saturday the 21st at 10am, Crestview fields, if you want to come. He's been asking whether you'd make it to a game all spring, so I wanted you to have it on the calendar with enough notice this time.

On the Father's
subject: Donna

body_preview:
Kelly,

I'll be at that game, chair is already in the car. Tell Owen I'm sitting on the home side this time so I can actually see his face when he scores.

Dinner on the 22nd is perfect. I'm doing pot roast, nothing complicated. And I'll set Nora up at t
subject: Re: Donna

body_preview:
Sharon to Kelly
Subject: Jaylen and his teacher, need your school brain for a sec

Hey Kel,

I know you're in end-of-year crunch mode but I need five minutes of your counselor brain. Jaylen's been coming home in a mood for three weeks and last night it f
subject: Sharon to Kelly

body_preview:
Kelly to Sharon
Subject: Re: Jaylen and his teacher, need your school brain for a sec

Shar,

Don't go to the principal yet.

Here's what I'd tell the parent across from my desk: get Jaylen's version documented. Write down specific inci
subject: Re: Kelly to Sharon

body_preview:
Sharon to Kelly
Subject: Re: Jaylen and his teacher, need your school brain for a sec

Kel,

I emailed Denton Tuesday night, exactly the way you said, short, neutral, 'checking in' energy. He replied within the hour (surprised me) and we had a ten-minut
subject: Re: Sharon to Kelly

body_preview:
Renee,

Donna called Sunday to say she's coming November 9th and staying "through the month." Greg delivered this like a weather update. He seemed pleased.

I love her. I do. But Nora has swim camp the second week, Owen has robotics at the Y, and end-of-year
subject: Renee

body_preview:
Kelly,

"Through the month" is doing a lot of work in that sentence. Has Greg done the math? That's 22 days.

Thursday works. I'll book that Italian place on Patterson, far enough from Henrico that you won't run into a student's parent. Wear something co
subject: Re: Kelly

body_preview:
Renee,

Thursday helped more than I can explain, thank you.

Donna arrived Saturday. The linen closet has been reorganized. She has made her feelings about our throw pillows known. Greg finds all of this charming. Owen doesn't notice anything. Nora has s
subject: Re: Renee

body_preview:
Kelly,

"Nora has started eating breakfast in her room", that child is going to navigate adulthood with zero difficulty.

The early-departure strategy is completely legitimate. You spend your days helping twelve-year-olds survive actual crises. You are a
subject: Re: Kelly
